name: fresh
description: Terminal based IDE & text editor: easy, powerful and fast
readme_summary: Fresh is a modern, full-featured text editor designed for the terminal, offering IDE-level features like multi-cursor editing, a command palette, and LSP support with zero configuration. It prioritizes performance, handling large files efficiently and providing a familiar user experience akin to graphical editors.
primary_use_case: To provide a powerful, fast, and user-friendly text editing experience directly within the terminal, suitable for developers who prefer a terminal-based workflow or need to handle large files efficiently.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled artisan in a cozy, dimly lit workshop, meticulously crafting a complex, glowing mechanical clockwork device entirely out of digital code and light. The artisan uses a magnifying glass that reveals intricate circuit patterns within the gears, symbolizing the IDE's detailed features. Input scrolls of raw data feed into a slot on the workbench, and the finished clockwork, representing a perfectly structured document, emerges. Subtle UI elements like a command palette and a file explorer are integrated into the workshop's background. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with a warm, inviting color palette and soft, focused lighting, emphasizing precision and ease of use.

name: intercept
description: iNTERCEPT, a free and open-source platform that unites the best signal intelligence tools into a single, accessible interface.
readme_summary: iNTERCEPT is an open-source, web-based platform that integrates a wide array of signal intelligence tools into a unified interface. It supports various signal decoding, tracking, and analysis functionalities, making advanced SIGINT capabilities accessible.
primary_use_case: To provide a comprehensive and integrated platform for signal intelligence (SIGINT) gathering, analysis, and decoding using various software-defined radio (SDR) and related tools.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled radio operator, depicted as a modern-day cartographer, standing at a large, interactive globe. Instead of drawing maps, they are using a specialized, glowing console to draw lines and place markers representing various signals emanating from different points on the globe. The console displays real-time data streams, satellite paths, and decoded messages, visually showing the capture and analysis of diverse radio transmissions like aircraft, ships, and weather patterns. Subtle UI elements of the 'iNTERCEPT' platform are visible on the console screens. The scene is set in a high-tech, dimly lit command center with a focus on the central operator and the illuminated globe. The style is a 3D isometric illustration with a futuristic, clean aesthetic and a color palette of blues, greens, and subtle oranges.

name: zen
description: Selfhosted notes app. Single golang binary, notes stored as markdown within SQLite, full-text search, very low resource usage
readme_summary: Zen is a self-hosted notes application that stores notes as Markdown files in a local SQLite database. It offers features like full-text search, flexible tagging, and data portability, all within a single, low-resource binary or Docker container.
key_features: Single Go binary or Docker Compose
Standard Markdown files, local SQLite database
Import and export data with full portability
primary_use_case: A self-hosted, private note-taking and knowledge management application.
cover_image_prompt: A serene, minimalist library where each book represents a note. A gentle, glowing thread connects related books, symbolizing tags and full-text search. A wise, calm librarian (representing the user) is effortlessly organizing these books on shelves, with some books subtly glowing with important information. In the background, a small, efficient digital interface shows data flowing smoothly into and out of a secure vault (representing the SQLite database). The overall mood is peaceful and organized, with soft, natural lighting. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, modern aesthetic, emphasizing clarity and order.

name: Jauth
description: Lightweight SSL/TLS reverse proxy with authorization(via Telegram and SSH) for self-hosted apps
readme_summary: Jauth is a Go-based, lightweight SSL/TLS reverse proxy designed to secure self-hosted applications. It provides authorization through SSH or Telegram and supports Single Sign-On (SSO), simplifying access management for internal services.
key_features: User authorization via SSH or Telegram
primary_use_case: Securing and managing access to self-hosted applications through a lightweight reverse proxy with flexible authorization methods.
cover_image_prompt: A digital gatekeeper, depicted as a stylized, glowing key, standing guard before a series of diverse, colorful doors representing self-hosted applications. The key is simultaneously receiving authentication signals from a miniature Telegram logo and a stylized SSH key icon. Successful authentications cause the doors to glow invitingly. Subtle lines of code and data streams flow around the key, indicating its role as a secure intermediary. The background is a clean, modern interface with the name 'Jauth' subtly integrated. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with a vibrant, secure color palette, emphasizing clarity and protection.

name: GitJournal
description: Mobile first Note Taking integrated with Git
readme_summary: GitJournal is a mobile-first note-taking application that prioritizes privacy and data portability by storing notes in a standardized Markdown format within a Git repository. Users can choose to self-host their notes or use various Git providers like GitHub and GitLab.
key_features: Supports GitHub, GitLab, and custom Git providers
Focus on privacy and data portability
primary_use_case: A private and portable note-taking solution integrated with Git for version control and flexible hosting.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, open book made of digital pages, with each page seamlessly transforming into a Git commit graph as it's turned. A hand is shown writing notes on one page using a stylus, and the notes instantly appear as formatted Markdown text with YAML headers. Subtle digital particles representing data flow connect the writing hand to the Git commits. The background is a clean, modern workspace with a subtle GitJournal logo on a monitor. The overall mood is organized and secure, with a color palette of blues, greens, and grays. Rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with clear, crisp lines and soft lighting.

name: Monic
description: ddc ci utility for linux which live in you tray. Brightnress, sound and input.
readme_summary: Monic is a Python-based utility for Linux that provides system tray control over external monitor settings like brightness, volume, and input source. It features smooth animations for brightness changes and supports multiple monitors with an automatic update mechanism.
key_features: Volume control for built-in monitor speakers
primary_use_case: Controlling external monitor settings (brightness, volume, input) directly from the system tray on Linux.
cover_image_prompt: A digital conductor standing at a sleek, futuristic control panel that resembles a system tray. With elegant gestures, the conductor manipulates glowing sliders and dials, which in turn control the brightness and input source of multiple monitors elegantly arranged behind them. Subtle visual cues like flowing data streams and small, embedded UI elements on the control panel indicate the software's function. The overall scene is clean, modern, and uses a cool color palette with glowing accents, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style to emphasize control and precision.
